skill.name: pollinations-ai
skill.description: Generate images with Pollinations.ai using URL prompts, optional parameters, Python automation, batch variations, and saved metadata.
skill.summary: Generate and save Pollinations.ai images from structured prompts and simple scripts.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: The skill intentionally sends prompts to the Pollinations.ai image API and writes downloaded image data to local files. The static weak-crypto, path-traversal, and most backtick execution findings are false positives from Markdown, prose, and related-skill links. No prompt injection attempt, credential access, hidden exfiltration, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External Image API Requests
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs users to send image prompts to https://image.pollinations.ai through curl and Python requests. This is expected for the skill, but prompts are transmitted to an external service and may contain sensitive project details.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoint and request method are explicit in both skill files. The behavior is legitimate for image generation, but it is still a real external data disclosure path.

medium_findings.title: Local File Writes From Downloaded Content
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The Python example writes response content to output_file and writes metadata to generation_metadata.json. This is expected behavior, but unchecked output paths could overwrite local files if copied into automation without validation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly opens user-supplied output_file in write-binary mode and writes remote response content. The risk is bounded because the skill presents examples, not hidden executable code.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Examples Require User Review
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill includes curl, bash, and ask-gemini examples. They appear instructional and aligned with the skill purpose, but users should review generated scripts before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible examples rather than concealed execution. They still represent external command usage when a user follows the instructions.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Most Ruby or shell backtick findings come from Markdown code fences, inline parameter names, tags, and example formatting. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples or inline text. There is no Ruby code path or backtick-based command execution syntax used as executable logic.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Are Relative Documentation Links
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The ../ sequences appear in related-skill Markdown links, not in file access logic. No evidence found that these paths are opened, executed, or used for filesystem traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown links under a Related Skills section. They do not form a runtime path traversal vector.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography alerts match normal prose, style text, design wording, or color guidance. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic code use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited context shows image-prompt guidance and metadata examples, not cryptographic functions. The static pattern is not supported semantically.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system and network reconnaissance alerts occur in normal use-case and validation wording. No evidence found of host discovery, port scanning, environment enumeration, or network probing commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe creative use cases and workflow validation. They contain no reconnaissance utilities, command patterns, or collection of system details.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP Request To External Generation Service
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill constructs Pollinations.ai URLs and sends prompt text over the network. Users should avoid including confidential project, client, or personal data in prompts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL and requests.get call are explicit. The data path is clear and intentional.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Response Written To Local Output Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The sample code writes downloaded response bytes to a caller-provided file path. Users should constrain output paths to a project asset directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path is direct and visible. Impact depends on how users adapt the output_file argument.

content.value_statement: Teams often need fast visual assets without configuring paid image tools. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Pollinations.ai image generation, saving, batching, and metadata tracking.
actual_capabilities: Explains the Pollinations.ai image URL format and supported query parameters.
Shows how to generate images from browser, curl, and Python workflows.
Provides a Python helper for saving generated image files locally.
Supports batch generation with multiple prompts or fixed seeds.
Documents output metadata for prompt, model, dimensions, seed, and file name.
Suggests prompt structures for hero images, thumbnails, banners, and style variants.
limitations: Generated prompts are sent to an external Pollinations.ai service.
Image quality and model availability depend on the public API.
The examples do not validate output paths before writing files.
The skill does not perform image editing, masking, or local model inference.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Visuals
use_cases.description: Create quick placeholder images for websites, app screens, and product mockups before final assets are ready.

use_cases.title: Generate Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Produce several hero, banner, or social media concepts with consistent dimensions and reusable prompt metadata.

use_cases.title: Automate Visual Variations
use_cases.description: Use seeds and batch prompts to create controlled image variations for creative review or testing.

prompt_templates.title: Create One Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate one Pollinations.ai prompt for a 16:9 website hero image about [topic]. Include model, width, height, and a safe file name.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Prompt Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for multiple creative directions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three Pollinations.ai image prompts for [campaign]. Vary style, lighting, and composition. Keep dimensions at [width] by [height].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Batch Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a batch plan for Pollinations.ai with five prompts, fixed dimensions, model choice, seeds, output file names, and metadata fields.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Reproducible Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow request for repeatable production.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reproducible Pollinations.ai workflow for [asset type]. Include prompt conventions, seed strategy, output naming, metadata tracking, and review criteria.

output_examples.input: Create a website hero image for a calm productivity app.
output_examples.output: A complete image brief with a calm workspace prompt, 1920x1080 dimensions, the flux model, a fixed seed, and a suggested hero-image.jpg output name.

output_examples.input: Generate social media banner variations for a SaaS launch.
output: Three banner prompts with different visual styles.
A short metadata checklist for later review.

output_examples.input: Make product thumbnail concepts for a dashboard tool.
output_examples.output: A set of square thumbnail directions with prompt details, model selection, output file names, and notes about consistent lighting.

best_practices: Remove confidential client, product, or personal data before sending prompts to the API.
Keep generated files inside a dedicated asset folder with reviewed output names.
Record prompts, dimensions, model, seed, and selected outputs for repeatable work.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, unreleased strategy, or private user data into image prompts.
Do not run generated scripts before checking URLs, file paths, and overwrite behavior.
Do not expect exact brand compliance without reviewing and refining generated images.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Pollinations.ai API key?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes a public URL-based API that does not require signup or an API key.

faq.question: Can it save generated images?
faq.answer: Yes. The Python example downloads the response and writes it to a local image file.

faq.question: Can it create repeatable results?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends fixed seeds and saved metadata to improve reproducibility.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private prompts?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts are sent to an external service, so sensitive data should be removed first.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it edit existing images?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow focuses on text-to-image generation, saved files, and metadata.
